pages.title: College of Business | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Business (COB) offers a broad, professional education across 8 concentrations and 5 minors. We proudly schedule around the full- and part-time demands of its 4,500 undergraduate majors and 400 graduate students.
headings: College of Business California State University, Sacramento
Welcome to the College of Business at Sacramento State
Connect With The College of Business
contentBlocks: As the business arm of California’s Capital University, we develop engaged, responsible, and inclusive leaders who enrich their communities. Our students earn a rigorous, transformative management education.

The College of Business (COB) currently enrolls 4,000 students, with 3,500 at the undergraduate level and 500 at the graduate level. The College of Business has been accredited by the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) since 1963, one of just 5% of the world’s 16,000 higher education institutions to have earned this designation. The COB is composed of six departments and offers one undergraduate degree (BS in Business) with 10 concentrations, a Business Honors program, and six graduate degrees. The college is also in the process of developing three new graduate programs.

The COB has established itself as a leader in innovative programming, such as Business Analytics, Cybersecurity Management, and Digital Marketing. The College of Business has a strong reputation in quantitative business topics, including Accounting, Finance, and Analytics. The COB Office of Student Engagement provides excellent support to students, including advising, tutoring, professional development, and networking opportunities. The COB has 14 business student clubs and provides over $300k in scholarships for our business students.
Equal Opportunity and Excellence in Education and Employment

pages.title: Undergraduate Programs | Sacramento State
pages.description: The College of Business delivers a comprehensive & professional business education, offering a Bachelor of Science degree with 10 concentrations to suit students’ career goals.
contentBlocks: The College of Business (COB) Office of Student Engagement is here to support business students along the entire process of the business student experience at Sac State; from the time you are thinking about applying to the major all the way to when you are graduating — and all the stops along the way.
The College of Business offers a broad professional education in Business. The Bachelor of Science program offers students a choice of 10 concentrations within the business administration major.

pages.title: About the Undergraduate Business program | Sacramento State
pages.description: Academic program information for business majors.
headings: Sequencing of Upper Division Business Major courses matter!
contentBlocks: The College of Business has approximately 4,000 undergraduate business students whom we advise from impacted admissions process through graduation.
Its COB Office of Student Engagement provides accurate, thorough, and concise information with efficiency. Ensuring the timely graduation of our students is a great priority.

All business students must take the major core classes as part of their Business Administration degree. In order to be eligible to enroll in the majority of the major core classes a student must be admitted into the upper division major.

Major core classes should be taken concurrently with concentration courses. Many concentrations have prerequisites from the Major Core. All courses in the lower division/computer literacy requirement, major core and concentrations need to be completed with a minimum C- grade while maintaining a 2.0 GPA in each area.

The College of Business has policies and procedures that are unique to our college. It is the responsibility of the student to be aware of the policies. Please visit the following link for the 3 most common policies. CBA Policies and Procedures.
24 units of coursework in the major core apply to all concentrations.

Students in all concentrations should be aware of and try to take the "gateway" courses in their area as soon as possible, as they impede your ability to progress through the program in a timely manner. Prerequisite requirements for required major core and concentration classes are typically noted in parenthesis in the University Catalog.
The College of Business (COB) offers a broad, professional education in business. Students working toward the BS degree must select at least one of the 10 concentrations that are offered.

In addition to concentration courses, Business Administration students must also complete the Major Core requirements . Concentration courses should be taken concurrently with the Major Core after you enter the major. Many concentrations have prerequisites from the Major Core. In order to advance into the upper division major/concentration, students are required to complete the computer literacy and lower division business requirements. You must submit an application to the upper division major through the Impaction process to move into the upper division major/concentration.
Concentration courses must be completed with a minimum C- grade, while maintaining a 2.0 average in the concentration.
ACCOUNTANCY CONCENTRATION (through Spring 2025)
ACCOUNTANCY CONCENTRATION (effective Fall 2025)
AI MANAGEMENT CONCENTRATION (effective Fall 2026)
FINANCE CONCENTRATION: GENERAL TRACK FINANCIAL PLANNING TRACK REAL ESTATE TRACK RISK MANAGEMENT TRACK
MGMT OF HUMAN RESOURCES & ORGANIZATIONAL BEHAVIOR CONCENTRATION
MANAGEMENT INFORMATION SYSTEMS CONCENTRATION

The College of Business offers various options for student wishing to pursue a minor in Business Administration. The first of the minors offered is a 24 unit minor in Business Administration for a student not majoring in Business Administration. The remaining minors are specialized business minors that are open to business majors and majors outside of Business Administration. A business student may complete a specialized minor as long as it is not the same as their concentration.
AI MANAGEMENT MINOR (effective Fall 2026)
ENTREPRENEURSHIP MINOR (effective Fall 2026)
MGMT OF HUMAN RESOURCES & ORGANIZATIONAL BEHAVIOR MINOR
A student wishing to minor in the College of Business must file a minor request petition.
Whether you began your college career at Sacramento State or transferred from another college (or colleges), all students are required to satisfy:
University General Education & Graduation requirements

Students should familiarize themselves with these requirements as soon as possible in order to plan their college careers. Current General Education and Graduation requirements can be found in the University Catalog .

One way to check on your General Education progress is to review your Academic Requirements in your Student Center . This report attempts to assess your progress in both General Education and in your Major. Please keep in mind that the report may not always accurately read coursework for the Business major if the courses were taken at another institution (or if the transcripts have not been received or were recently received).

If you have questions about General Education Academic Requirements, please see Academic Advising in Lassen Hall. Questions related to the Business major should be directed to the Undergraduate Advising Center in Tahoe Hall 1030, (916) 278-2499.

Please keep in mind that at least 9 units of General Education (3 courses) must be completed at the Upper Division level (approved junior/senior level classes at Sacramento State numbered "100-199"). The list of approved courses can be found in the University Catalog.

This means that even if you completed all of your General Education "Area" requirements (A,B,C,D,E) at another college, at least 3 more courses must be completed at Sacramento State (courses must be approved General Education courses numbered 100-199). At least one of the 3 courses must be an approved "Writing Intensive" course.
All lower division and upper division business courses must be passed with a C- or better with the exception of MATH 24 which requires a minimum acceptable grade of “C”.

Students whose Sac State or overall GPA falls below 2.0 may be disqualified from the business major. As a result, they will not be able to enroll in any business courses until they have been readmitted.

The Academic Standards Policy of the College of Business requires all disqualified students to reapply (i.e., submit an application) to the business major once they are back in good standing with the university. Students seeking readmission will be ranked by their Sacramento State and lower division business GPAs among all other applicants through the impaction process.

It is rare for a business major to be short of the 120 unit requirement for graduation with a bachelor’s degree. It may happen in rare cases when a student has been very efficient choosing general education courses along with their major courses. In order to be sure you will reach 120 units, all you need to do it determine the units you have remaining and then add that to the number of units you have completed.

You can see how many units you have completed by reviewing your Unofficial Transcript in your Student Center . Be sure to account for repeats and transfer credit that might not yet have transferred when calculating your units.

College of Business lower division and upper division major requirements may be repeated twice (for a total of three [3] attempts – 2nd and 3rd attempts will be averaged). If a required course is not completed with a minimum acceptable grade in three attempts, the student may not continue in the business major.
Upper Division courses used to satisfy graduation requirements must have been completed within ten (10) years of the date of graduation.

pages.title: Dean's Honors List | Sacramento State
pages.description: Dean's Honors List of honors students at the College of Business at Sacramento State
headings: College of Business California State University, Sacramento
Equal Opportunity and Excellence in Education and Employment
contentBlocks: Semester Honors are awarded and the notation "Deans Honors List" is posted to the permanent academic record for freshmen earning a 3.0 GPA and other undergraduates earning a 3.25 GPA. To be eligible in either category, students must complete a minimum of 12 letter-graded units with no incomplete grades and no grade lower than a C-.
Graduate students are not eligible for the Dean's Honors List.

All university programs and activities are open and available to all regardless of race, sex, color, ethnicity or national origin. Consistent with California law and federal civil rights laws, the California State University, Sacramento provides equal opportunity in education and employment without unlawful discrimination or preferential treatment based on race, sex, color, ethnicity, or national origin. Our commitment to equal opportunity means ensuring that every student and employee has access to the resources and support they need to thrive and succeed in a university environment and in their communities. The California State University, Sacramento complies with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act, the California Equity in Higher Education Act, California’s Proposition 209 (Art. I, Section 31 of the California Constitution), other applicable state and federal anti-discrimination laws, and CSU’s Nondiscrimination Policy. We prohibit discriminatory preferential treatment, segregation based on race or any other protected status, and all forms of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation in all university programs, policies, and practices.

The California State University, Sacramento is a diverse community of individuals who represent many perspectives, beliefs and identities, committed to fostering an inclusive, respectful, and intellectually vibrant environment. We cultivate a culture of open dialogue, mutual respect, and belonging to support educational excellence and student success. Through academic programs, student organizations and activities, faculty initiatives, and community partnerships, we encourage meaningful engagement with diverse perspectives. As a higher education institution, we are dedicated to advancing knowledge and empowering individuals to reach their full potential by prioritizing inclusive curriculum development, faculty and staff training, student mentorship, and comprehensive support programs. At the California State University, Sacramento, excellence is built on merit, talent, diversity, accessibility, and equal opportunity for all.
Equal Opportunity and Excellence in Education and Employment

pages.title: Business Advisory Council | Sacramento State
pages.description: The Business Advisory Council (BAC) provides input and assistance to the College of Business (COB) as it seeks to fulfill its mission, including issues related to resource development, external relations, and strategic planning. The BAC is comprised of alumni, community leaders, corporate partners, and other friends of the University. Individuals selected have demonstrated leadership in their chosen field, and are committed to the mission, vision, and success of the COB.
headings: College of Business California State University, Sacramento
contentBlocks: The Business Advisory Council (BAC) provides input and assistance to the College of Business (COB) as it seeks to fulfill its mission, including issues related to resource development, external relations, and strategic planning. The BAC is comprised of alumni, community leaders, corporate partners, and other friends of the University. Individuals selected have demonstrated leadership in their chosen field, and are committed to the mission, vision, and success of the COB.

Suman Akula, is a serial entrepreneur, author, and philanthropist. His innate curiosity led him to pursue engineering in India and a master's degree in Computer Science from the University of Bridgeport, USA. Inspired by the entrepreneurial spirit, Suman ventured into the start-up world, successfully building multiple technology companies: Rapid Application Developers LLC, [s]Cube Inc, Sageable Technologies, SmartBridge Educational Pvt. Ltd., and StackNexus Inc. These ventures have created employment for over 2000 personnel globally.

In addition to his entrepreneurial pursuits, Suman is an accomplished author, having penned "HIT - Hustle, Impact, Tenacity". Suman's philanthropic efforts are channeled through the Akula Foundation, which is on a mission to eradicate poverty and child illiteracy in India. He is focused on giving back to the community.
Senior Vice President in Wealth Management

John grew up in Sacramento. After graduating college, John joined Merrill Lynch where he established a financial advisory team helping individuals with their financial and planning needs. He and his team relocated from Merrill Lynch to another firm in 2016. John is a co-founder and board member of the Sacramento Angels (a private investment group) and current managing partner at Lakeside Equestrian Center. He is the former founder, president and CEO of Loomis Tank Lines, Inc., former president of A&H Ventures, past member of EO and former vice chair of the Sacramento Ballet. John is involved in motorsports, he is a retired member of DDMNC and was the past director of the Hangtown MX Classic Outdoor National and still assist in making that event a success. He graduated from CSUS in 1995 with a BS in Accounting.

Mr. Beckwith has served as Five Star Bank’s President and Chief Executive Officer since 2003. Mr. Beckwith is deeply connected to the Sacramento community and has chaired several community-based organizations including the Sacramento Metro Chamber of Commerce, Valley Vision and KVIE (the local PBS affiliate). Mr. Beckwith is currently the Chair of the Western Bankers Association, a Private Sector Director with Greater Sacramento Economic Council, a board member of Crocker Art Museum and a member of the Sacramento State University College of Business Advisory Council. He graduated from San Francisco State University with a Bachelor of Science in Business Administration with a concentration in Accounting. He is also a graduate of Pacific Coast Banking School at the University of Washington where he was class president. In 2020, Mr. Beckwith was recognized by the Sacramento Metro Chamber of Commerce as the Businessman of the Year. He was named among the Most Admired CEO's of 2020 by the Sacramento Business Journal. In 2021, Five Star Bank received the 2021 Sacramento Asian Pacific Chamber Corporate Steward Award and the 2021 Corporate Champion of the Year Award from the Sacramento Business Journal.
Vice President of Enterprise Analytics at Altais Health

James Berkery is currently the Vice President of Enterprise Analytics at Altais Health, an organization dedicated to activating an exceptional healthcare system that cultivates the health and well-being of physicians, patients, and the clinical community. He leads teams in Actuarial Services, Financial Planning, and Healthcare Analytics. A healthcare industry veteran, James has served in different roles across the care continuum for nearly 20 years in hospitals, insurers and providers spanning operations, finance, and technology roles. He is active with the American College of Healthcare Executives (ACHE), currently pursuing a fellowship and the Healthcare Financial Management Association (HFMA). James is a proud product of California public universities, graduating from University of California - San Diego (UCSD) with a degree in Economics and completed his Master’s in Business Administration at California State University Sacramento. He has had the privilege to serve as a mentor through the UCSD Alumni Association and industry programs. He is grateful and excited to have the opportunity to contribute to the Business Advisory Council. He resides in Granite Bay, with his wife, an Oncology Nurse, and two children. Outside of work, he is very engaged in his children's scholastic and sporting activities.

Paul Coccovillo is the CFO of Rhombus Systems, Inc., a next generation video security company that is working on blending A.I. and machine learning to advance the field of safety. Paul has over 20 years of finance experience and progressive leadership positions throughout the tech industry in Silicon Valley having worked in 4 other start-ups previously. He joined Rhombus Systems in 2021. Before joining Rhombus Systems Paul was with Unicorn companies Oculus VR, Juul Labs, and held roles at Meta (Previously Facebook), and Microsoft. Paul has an undergraduate degree in Accountancy from Bentley University in Waltham, MA, and an MBA in Corporate Finance from California State University, Sacramento.
Senior Director of Operations, Quick Quack Car Wash

Kyle Desautels is an operations leader who brings over 12 years of experience leading large teams in manufacturing, fulfillment, logistics, and retail operations. Kyle is a Lean Six Sigma black belt, holds a Bachelor of Science in Industrial Technology, a Master of Science in Engineering Management, and an MBA from Cal Poly San Luis Obispo. Throughout his career, Kyle has led packaging manufacturing operations in the San Francisco Bay area that pioneered digital print capabilities in corrugated packaging, led fulfillment center operations for Amazon in their robotic facilities, and led the growth of Amazon’s logistics network on the West Coast before the pandemic. Currently, Kyle leads all facilities, maintenance, and store operations in the United States for Quick Quack Car Wash as the Senior Director of Operations. In this role, he loves being able to pursue his passions of leadership development and process improvement in an environment that puts people and culture first.

K-deep received a Bachelors degree in Business Administration (Accountancy) from CSU Sacramento in 2005. After working in finance and operations in the wine industry from 2005 to 2007, K-deep joined the firm of Moss Adams where he currently serves as a leader of the audit practice for the Northern California region of the firm as well as the Agribusiness practice leader for California. K-deep leads a team of over 150 auditors, CPAs, and agribusiness experts. In 2021, K-deep was recognized as a member of the Sacramento Business Journal’s 40 under 40 class. K-deep resides in Folsom, CA with his wife and their 3 children.
President and Founder of Estrada Consulting, Inc. (ECI)

Rafael Estrada is the President and Founder of Estrada Consulting, Inc. (ECI), a leading IT consulting company based in Sacramento. Under Rafael's leadership, ECI's senior teams have delivered IT solutions to over 35 state organizations spanning more than 20 years. Recently, ECI expanded its operations to Mexico and opened an office in Guadalajara to support nearshore development projects. ECI specializes in various areas including business intelligence, application development, enterprise architecture, project and program management, as well as COTS (Commercial Off-The-Shelf) and software solutions. Rafael holds a Bachelor's degree in Finance from Sacramento State, which he earned in 1995. He further pursued his education and obtained an MBA in 1998. Additionally, he actively participates in Sacramento State's Professional Pathways Networking Program, where he offers business leadership mentoring to current students.
Vice President of Marketing, Sky River Casino

Carrie Gordon oversees brand, service and product marketing, advertising, guest acquisition and retention, analytics, and digital products for Sky River Casino. Her career spans nearly twenty years, specializing in a breadth of product and service marketing functions for highly regulated industries. She is passionate about the data-driven evolution of the meaningful, personalized relationships developed between a brand and it’s audiences. Outside of work, Carrie enjoys spending time at her kids’ sports games, camping, traveling, and staying as active as possible. Raised in Davis, she earned her BA in Mass Media Communications as well as her Executive Masters of Business Administration from Sac State.
HR Organizational Training and Development Manager, InterWest Insurance Services

Dana is a Senior Human Resources leader and business partner who brings over thirty years of managerial, operational, and human resource expertise. Dana holds a Bachelor of Science in Personnel and Industrial Relations from San Francisco State University and a Master of Science degree in Human Resources Management from Golden Gate University. She has worked in both the non-profit and for-profit sectors in Environmental Protection, Real Estate, Telecommunications, and in Insurance.

Dana worked for the Sierra Club for fourteen years and served as the Director of Employee Relations at their headquarters office in San Francisco. In 2008 she moved to Sacramento and married her husband Bob who is a Sacramento State Alumni. After moving to the Sacramento area, she served as Director of Human Resources for InterWest Insurance Services until 2019 when she changed her role to focus on her love of training, development, and oversight of their internship program. The internship program has successfully introduced many students from universities such as Sacramento State, Chico State, and Sonoma State to a career in insurance.

As a HR Organizational Training and Development Manager, Dana champions student entry and career development in our business community. She takes pride in designing and implementing strategies and programs to motivate, engage, and educate a committed high performing workforce.

David J. Lopez is a professional investor focused on public markets and startups. He previously worked as a portfolio manager managing investment selection and allocation for ultra-high net worth individuals, foundations, and institutional clients, and on buy-side investment teams overseeing billions in assets.
David graduated from Sacramento State with a Bachelor's Degree in Finance and a minor in Government, where he served as President of the Student Investment Fund, a program he remains engaged with.

He currently serves as a board member at the University Foundation at Sacramento State where he leverages his background on the Finance Committee, and funds the David J. Lopez Scholarship for Diversity in Financial Services which provides financial support to high achieving students from historically underrepresented backgrounds. David is also an active member of the Sacramento Angels, serving on the deal selections team. While in college, he was also appointed to a Sacramento County planning advisory council where he served as Co-Chairman.
Senior Vice President | Northern California Regional Hub Manager US Bank

Todd is an accomplished executive with 27 years of experience in commercial real estate and banking, specializing in loan underwriting, portfolio management, and business development. Currently serving as Senior Vice President at US Bank in Sacramento, Todd oversees a team responsible for managing a $3.1 billion commercial real estate loan portfolio in Northern California. Todd plays a pivotal role in structuring and underwriting loans, collaborating with credit officers and relationship managers to ensure optimal portfolio performance and adherence to credit policies.

Prior to his current role, Todd held various influential positions at Union Bank including Director of Syndication/Mezzanine Loans, Commercial Real Estate Loan Workout Officer and Relationship Manager. Throughout the career, Todd has been dedicated to optimizing portfolio performance, mitigating risk, and ensuring sustainable growth.

Todd is also a respected lecturer at the UC Davis Graduate School of Management, teaching a commercial real estate finance course at one of the nation’s top business schools. Todd holds an MBA and a Bachelor of Science in Managerial Economics from UC Davis.
SVP/Chief Operations Officer and Chief DE&I Officer, Five Star Bank

Ms. Ramirez has extensive experience in senior level banking and is skilled in strategic operational process and sales management. Ms. Ramirez serves on the board of directors of the Sacramento Hispanic Chamber and the Sacramento Hispanic Chamber PAC. She also serves on the board of directors of the Sacramento Black Chamber and the Sacramento Food Bank & Family Services. She also serves on the board of directors of the Cal Aggie Alumni Association and is a member of the Sacramento State University College of Business Advisory Council. In 2022, Ms. Ramirez was recognized by the Sacramento Business Journal among Women Who Means Business honorees and was among the Sacramento Bee’s inaugural class of Top 25 Latino Change Makers. Ms. Ramirez graduated from UC Davis, with a BS in Psychology and BA in Spanish and from Sacramento State, with an MBA. She is also a graduate of CBA Executive Banking School.
Senior Vice President and Head of Community Banking, Tri Counties Bank

Scott serves as Senior Vice President and Head of Community Banking for Tri Counties Bank. He is responsible for all branch banking, home lending, wealth management, customer support and direct banking teams. Tri Counties Bank serves communities throughout California with assets of $9.8 billion and a fifty-year record of financial success and stability. Robertson has over 32 years of experience in the financial services industry, starting his career as a teller while attending Sacramento State.

He serves on several non-profit boards in the Sacramento region, including the Center for Fathers and Families, the Salvation Army and Broadway Sacramento. Scott graduated from Sacramento State with a BS in Business/Marketing. He is also a graduate of the CBA Executive Banking School at the University of Virginia where he received “Jefferson Cup” honors recognition. Scott was honored by Sacramento State with the “Distinguished Alumni Award” in 2023. Scott and his wife Michelle have been married 30 years and have 2 adult children.
Chief Marketing Officer, Tipsy Putt Brands
Executive Officer, Franchise Tax Board of California

Selvi Stanislaus was appointed the Executive Officer of the California Franchise Tax Board (FTB) on January 11, 2006. As FTB’s fourth Executive Officer and the first woman to hold this position, Selvi oversees the second-largest tax department in the nation, with more than 6,000 employees in California and its three out-of-state offices in New York, Chicago and Houston. Under Selvi’s Leadership, the FTB has: • Increased development of a positive workplace culture that has made FTB a destination employer. • Successfully launched the Enterprise Data to Revenue (EDR) Project. The project’s one-time costs were approximately $522 million with over $5.1 billion in revenue collected by the FTB for the State of California. • Launched a new, streamlined version of FTB’s public website focused on an improved user experience. • Successfully implemented growth of Leadership Development Programs. These programs are a key component of FTB’s strategic commitment to invest in their employees. • Enhanced MyFTB, a FTB website tool that provides taxpayers and representatives access to more tax information, new self-service options, and an improved platform for communicating. • FTB effectively administered new tax programs such as the California Earned Income Tax Credit and Young Child Tax Credit which provide refundable (cash-back) credits for qualified lower income working Californians.

Tim retired in 2019 after 37 years of professional experience with Deloitte. Tim was the Managing Partner for Deloitte’s Sacramento office for many years and also held other leadership roles for the firm. While at Deloitte, Tim led the Firm’s services to many large public and private organizations, and advised his clients on numerous corporate finance matters, including mergers and acquisitions, and equity and bond offerings. Tim’s clients included some of the most well-known organizations in the US. Over the years, Tim has had extensive interaction with the governing boards of both public and private entities.
Since retiring, Tim has remained active in both his local and professional community, and he is a past president and current board member of several organizations.
Tim received his BS degree in accounting from California State University, Sacramento.
Chief People Officer, Quick Quack Car Wash

Kenny Williams is an 18-year Human Resources Professional. He started his Human Resources career at The Home Depot and has worked in different industries, such as manufacturing, distribution, and service. Kenny has been employed with Quick Quack Car Wash for 10 years, currently as the Chief People Officer. Kenny has played vital role in Quick Quack Car Wash’s growth, leading the company to maintain its culture, as it has grown from 16 car washes to the 200+ they have today. Kenny is responsible for employee relations, training, organizational development, recruiting, safety/compliance, and workforce planning. He received his B.S. in Business Administration (concentration in Human Resources), and B.A. in Communication, from California State University, Sacramento. He is a Senior Certified Human Resources Professional (SPHR) and a Society of Human Resource Management -Senior Certified Professional.

contentBlocks: The Center for Small Business no-cost services are provided by Sacramento State upper-division and graduate students under faculty supervision with the support of area financial institutions. The Center for Business Analytics is a lab that provides innovative solutions to our partners’ problemsin the "Big Data" economy through clever applications of cutting-edge modern analytics.

contentBlocks: One in 20 Sacramento residents are members of the Hornet family. The College of Business has produced over 40,000 graduates, approximately 24,000 of whom remain in the region. College of Business alumni can give back to their community by making a gift, hiring an intern or a graduate, or becoming a guest speaker. You can even sponsor a student scholarship.

pages.title: Employer Engagement Opportunities | Sacramento State
pages.description: We invite and welcome employers and organizations who wish to partner with us and engage with our business students. We provide a variety of ways for you to connect with our students.
headings: College of Business California State University, Sacramento
Biz Lounge Employer in Residence Series
Request to be a Guest Speaker in Business Courses
contentBlocks: Graduate Career Services (MBA, EMBA, IMBA, MSA)

We invite and welcome employers and organizations who wish to partner with us and engage with our business students. We provide a variety of unique, fun and meaningful ways for you to connect with our students.
Employers interested in recruiting students and alumni of our MBA, MBA for Executives, International MBA, and MS in Accountancy programs, visit Graduate Career Services.
The College of Business offers an Academic Internship program where students may opt to complete an internship for academic credit if they wish; however this is not required.

For an academic internship (student earns 3.0 credits in a semester), the company/organization must provide a supervised, paid experience that aligns with the student's concentration within the business major over the duration of an academic semester (approximately 150 hours over 15 weeks). For more details regarding academic internship requirements or to post an opportunity, please visit our Internship section on the Business Job Board. Academic Internship Information

Come hang out and network with our students in our new Biz Lounge! This is a great space to meet our students, and engage in easy, but meaningful ways. Share information about your company/organization, promote recruitment opportunities, establish or increase brand recognition, conduct interviews, invite faculty to meet you, review resumes and/or LinkedIn profiles, and more.

Our office coordinates all elements; your job is to simply be present and engage with our students. Your sponsorship includes a tailored approach to your day - we work with you to plan the food and refreshments (college students!), we market and promote your event, provide parking, and all of the important details.
You can select from a two hour event, half day or a full day. You select what works best for your needs.
If you are interested in participating in the Biz Lounge Employer in Residence series, please complete the interest form below.
I'm interested! Biz Lounge Employer in Residence Series

The Business Pathways Networking Event is a mini business conference for business students preparing to make a successful transition from campus to career. It is a networking and industry information experience to help our students' in their career readiness and management process. Business Pathways Networking Event has three main components:
Visit our Business Pathway Networking Event page for more event information.
If you are interested in participating in this event, please complete our Business Pathway Interest form.
There are opportunities each semester for companies and organizations to participate in career fairs to recruit Sac State business students.
The All-majors Career Fair is hosted by the Sac State Career Center and is offered in the fall semester and also in the spring semester. Advance registration is required via their website.

If you would like to request to be considered as a guest speaker in one or more business courses, it is a simple process. Complete the Guest Speaker Request form and once your information has been received, it is shared with all faculty in the College of Business Administration. Faculty will then review the information, and if it is a good fit with their course, faculty will reach out to you directly to coordinate a guest speaking opportunity.
If there are other ways you would like to partner with the COB to engage with our students, we would love to hear from you; please email us! Guest Speaker Request Form

Special Events for business students take place all throughout the academic year. We provide networking opportunities, career readiness events, information sessions with employers, and many different events designed to connect students with the local business community.
If you are an employer or organization who would like to engage with Sac State Business students, please review the wide variety of options to consider.

All of our events are designed to provide our students with the opportunity to interact with and network with companies and organizations where they may begin, build and engage in their own professional network and career development.

We invite partnerships with companies and organizations who are seeking to engage and meet our business students to sponsor a Study Break Event during the Fall or Spring semester. This format allows excellent interaction with our students as they drop by in between classes. You provide the food/beverage and we provide the students. This is very popular with our students as it supports them and fits in with their busy schedules.

A Study Break Event is perfect for organizations who are looking for more visibility and exposure on campus or for direct recruiting opportunities. For this type of event, the organization works with the COB Office of Student Engagement to provide a coffee break, lunch break or snack break for the students. The COB OSE provides event coordination, promotion to students and all publicity with your company sponsorship information. The cost varies based on the type of event hosted.
If you are interested in setting up a Study Break Event, please complete our Study Break Interest Form and our team will reach out to you to discuss possibilities. OSE Study Break Event Interest Form

pages.title: Important Operations Information | Sacramento State
pages.description: Important Operations Information
headings: College of Business California State University, Sacramento
contentBlocks: The College of Business, located in Tahoe Hall, is open and accessible to the public during normal business hours, Mon. – Fri. 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.

The best way to reach us is by email. We will all be checking our email regularly, so please continue to reach out to us with your questions. Our main office email account is cob@csus.edu . Any email sent there will be directed to the appropriate individual.
You can also reach us by phone at (916) 278-6578. Please leave a voicemail if we are unavailable and we will get back to you.

pages.title: Academic Advising for Business Students | Sacramento State
pages.description: Information for business majors regarding academic advising, major planning, status of degree.
headings: OSE Hours & Where to Find Us
Extended Drop-in Advising During Peak Registration
Scheduling an Appointment with an Advisor
How do I file to graduate?
When will I receive my diploma?
contentBlocks: Advising in the COB is a dual activity between the UBAC and the College's faculty.
Questions relating to concentrations and career information should be addressed to a faculty advisor.

The Office of Student Engagement will be holding in person and virtual office hours for students who have any questions pertaining to our services. Drop-in advising is designed t o assist students with quick questions, next semester scheduling, and any additional help that does not require a scheduled appointment.
Wednesday, April 29, 9:00 am - 12:00 p;m

Academic Advising in the Office of Student Engagement is available both in person and virtually. We are also happy to answer any questions for you via email. If you wish to reach out to more than one advisor please include all the advisors in one email rather than sending individual emails. Please see email addresses below:
If your LAST name begins with A - M please email Stephanie Serrato Neumann ( sjs256@csus.edu )
If your LAST name begins with N - Z please email Maria Lindstrom ( mariar@csus.edu )

UBAC counselors meet with students interested in finding out the status of their degree or in planning their curriculum, as well as students on Academic Probation. Only students who are currently enrolled as an upper division Business Major or "Expressed Interest" in Business on My Sac State may schedule an individual appointment with an Undergraduate Business Advising Center Academic Counselor. All other students who are not able to change their status to Expressed Interest may stop by the UBAC during scheduled zoom drop-in advising or ask quick questions during our virtual office hours.

Appointments are currently available virtually and in person. Appointments for business advising occur on a weekly basis. The appointments open on Fridays after 3:00 for the following week. If you have submitted a graduation application for Spring 2026 or Fall 2026 or are planning on submitting a graduation application for Spring 2026 or Fall 2026 you do not need to schedule a long term appointment. Please come to drop-in advising or email your advisor (see above). Please click on the link below to schedule the appointment.

There will be limited appointments April 13 - April 30. Appointments will be available with Peer Advisors only. The COB Advisors will be focusing on drop-in advising during the peak registration time to accommodate as many students as possible.
Please use the info below when scheduling an appointment through the link below:
Service: College of Business and choose Business Advising OR if you are Expressed Interest in Business you can choose EI in Business Advising
Pick a Date: Choose a date and will see available dates for the week
Schedule an appointment with a business advisor
If you have career questions of a specific concentration please visit a faculty advisor.
Transfer PowerPoint (Expressed Interest)
Apply to graduate online through Student Center on My Sac State once 85 units completed
Commencement information can be found at www. csus.edu/commencement
Students who are graduating in the summer may participate in Spring.
Your diploma will be mailed approximately 6 months after graduation. Students who need proof of their degree should order transcripts .

pages.title: Add/Drop Procedures After Second Week of Semester | Sacramento State
pages.description: How to add and drop College of Business Administration courses during the current virtual operations at Sacramento State.
headings: College of Business California State University, Sacramento
Add/Drop Procedures After Second Week of Semester
University Add Policy after 2nd Week of Semester
University Drop/Withdrawal Policy after 2nd Week of Semester
contentBlocks: The following instructions are for courses housed within the College of Business Administration.
Submit the petition via the University OnBase System (please review information emailed to your through you Saclink email address)
The form can be accessed in your Student Center on My Sac State in the Onbase Forms link (see image below)
If you choose to submit the form via this process the form will be routed automatically to the instructor and further authorizations. You will receive updates via your Sac State email.
Adds in weeks three and four of the semester are processed through the academic department and require approval by the instructor and department chair.

Adds are not approved after week four (the census date -- September 26, 2022) unless students present evidence of University error that prevented their timely registration. University error does not include failure to meet all payment deadlines. State your reasons for a late add request on a separate page and attach to this petition along with verification of the circumstances. Obtain signatures of the instructor, department chair, and college dean.
Drops during the third and fourth (through September 26, 2022) weeks of instruction are processed in the academic department offering the course and require instructor and department chair approval.
Drops after the fourth week of the semester (census date – September 26, 2022) are called withdrawals.
Withdrawals after the fourth week (September 27, 2022 and later) of the semester are granted only for “serious and compelling” reasons.

Withdrawal during the 5th and 6th week of the semester requires the signature of the course instructor and the department chair. Reasons for dropping in during this period include medical, carrying an excessive course load, student’s inadequate academic preparation for the course, or the student having significant job or career changes.

Withdrawal during the 7th through the 12th week requires the signature of the course instructor, the department chair, and the college dean. Reasons for withdrawal during this period include only medical or work related reasons clearly beyond the control of the student; a student initiated job change, carrying an excessive course load or inadequate preparation does not qualify.

Withdrawal is allowed after the 12th week of instruction only in exceptional cases, such as in cases of accident or serious illness where the cause is due to circumstances beyond the student’s control. All signatures are required and the student must meet with an Academic Advisor in the Academic Advising Center. Withdrawals approved during the last three weeks of the semester will not count towards the 18 unit maximum; however, a grade of “W” is still recorded on the transcript.
Please contact the Academic Programs Office:
For business advising, please contact the Office of StudentEngagement.
